verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease. It is synthesized with a signal peptide and pro-peptide, processed through the secretory pathway, and released as a soluble enzyme into the extracellular milieu (notably prostatic fluid and serum). It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable cell-surface anchoring mechanism. It is detected in serum/plasma as a soluble protein and used as a prostate cancer biomarker in that soluble form. There is no documented wash-resistant surface pool, no TM-partner tethering, and no cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display. It is a secreted-only protein.
